title: Strict OOF regime-aware stack plus beta calibration
summary: Public docs and calibration literature support stacking only from cross-validated base predictions, and beta calibration is often safer than plain Platt when score distributions are skewed.
rationale: Public docs and calibration literature support stacking only from cross-validated base predictions, and beta calibration is often safer than plain Platt when score distributions are skewed. For this task, different regimes likely need different calibrators because late-race forced stops and early-race non-events are distributed differently.
implementation_hint: Generate race-blocked OOF predictions from 3-4 diverse models only: current CatBoost/GBDT baseline, ranker, hazard sidecar, and optionally a simpler linear/GAM hazard. Train the stacker only on OOF predictions, then apply per-regime calibration: beta calibration on dense regimes, isotonic only where calibration sample size is large enough. Audit calibration separately for dry vs wet and early/mid/late race.
expected_effect: Usually low-risk when done strictly OOF; can convert small diverse signals into a stable AUC gain and cleaner submission probabilities.
risk: Main trap is leakage from using in-fold predictions or global calibrators fit on all data. Isotonic can overfit in sparse regimes, so gate it by sample count.
